model_narrative: We operate a small power grid over a single day broken into 24 hourly periods. In each hour we decide how much power to generate from wind, solar, and a fuel cell, how much power to send into or draw out of a battery, and whether the battery is set to charge or to discharge that hour. We also account for any demand we fail to serve and any surplus generation that has to be dumped. Generation, charging, discharging, unserved demand, and surplus each carry their own per-unit cost that can vary by hour. The objective is to minimize the total operating cost over the day.

sets.name: T
sets.doc: the hours of the day in chronological order, indexed by the integers 1 through 24; the first member is the opening hour and each later member follows the one before it

params.name: power_lim_wind
params.doc: the maximum wind power that can be generated in each hour, in power units

params.name: power_lim_pv
params.doc: the maximum solar (photovoltaic) power that can be generated in each hour, in power units

params.name: power_lim_fuel
params.doc: the maximum fuel-cell power that can be generated in each hour, in power units; equal to 80 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_charge
params.doc: the maximum power that can flow into the battery while charging in each hour, in power units; equal to 200 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_discharge
params.doc: the maximum power that can flow out of the battery while discharging in each hour, in power units; equal to 50 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_battery
params.doc: the maximum amount of energy the battery can hold in each hour, in power units; equal to 200 for every hour

params.name: initial_battery
params.doc: the amount of energy stored in the battery at the very start, before the first hour, in power units; a scalar equal to 100

params.name: load_demand
params.doc: the electricity load that must be served in each hour, in power units

params.name: cost_wind
params.doc: the unit cost of wind generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_pv
params.doc: the unit cost of solar generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_fuel
params.doc: the unit cost of fuel-cell generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_charge
params.doc: the unit cost of charging the battery in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_discharge
params.doc: the unit cost of discharging the battery in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_undelivered
params.doc: the penalty cost for each unit of demand left unserved in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_excess
params.doc: the unit cost charged for surplus generation that has to be dumped in each hour, in cost per power unit; equal to 0 for every hour

vars.name: wind
vars.doc: the wind power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: pv
vars.doc: the solar power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: fuel
vars.doc: the fuel-cell power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: charge
vars.doc: the power sent into the battery to charge it in each hour, in power units

vars.name: discharge
vars.doc: the power drawn out of the battery in each hour, in power units

vars.name: undelivered
vars.doc: the amount of demand left unserved in each hour, in power units

vars.name: excess
vars.doc: the surplus generation that exceeds what is needed and must be dumped in each hour, in power units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the discharge indicator for each hour; equals 1 if the battery is allowed to discharge that hour and 0 otherwise

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the charge indicator for each hour; equals 1 if the battery is allowed to charge that hour and 0 otherwise

vars.name: battery
vars.doc: the energy stored in the battery at the end of each hour, in power units

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total operating cost over the whole day, in cost units

constraints.name: wind_gen_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the wind power actually generated cannot exceed the most wind power that is available to generate that hour.

constraints.name: pv_gen_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the solar power actually generated cannot exceed the most solar power that is available to generate that hour.

constraints.name: fuel_gen_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the fuel-cell power actually generated cannot exceed the fuel cell's generation limit for that hour.

constraints.name: battery_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the energy held in the battery cannot exceed the battery's storage capacity for that hour.

constraints.name: discharge_constraint
constraints.intent: The battery can only draw power out when it is set to discharge that hour, and even then the power drawn out cannot exceed the discharging limit. In every hour the power discharged is held to zero unless the battery is in discharge mode, and when it is in discharge mode it is capped at the discharging limit for that hour.

constraints.name: charge_constraint
constraints.intent: The battery can only take power in when it is set to charge that hour, and even then the power taken in cannot exceed the charging limit. In every hour the power charged is held to zero unless the battery is in charge mode, and when it is in charge mode it is capped at the charging limit for that hour.

constraints.name: no_simultaneous_charge_discharge_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the battery cannot be set to charge and to discharge at the same time, so at most one of those two modes can be active in any given hour.

constraints.name: discharge_limit_by_battery_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the power sent into the battery added to the energy already stored coming into that hour cannot exceed the battery's storage capacity for that hour. The energy already stored coming into the hour is the level carried over from the end of the previous hour, except in the very first hour where it is the starting battery level.

constraints.name: state_of_battery_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the energy stored in the battery at the end of the hour equals the energy it held coming into the hour, reduced by whatever was discharged and increased by whatever was charged that hour. The energy coming into the hour is the level carried over from the end of the previous hour, except in the very first hour where it is the starting battery level.

constraints.name: power_balance_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour supply must match demand. The wind, solar, and fuel-cell generation together with the power discharged from the battery and any unserved demand must equal the load to be served plus the power used to charge the battery plus any surplus generation that is dumped.

constraints.name: total_cost_definition
constraints.intent: The total operating cost is the sum over all hours of the cost of every activity that hour: wind, solar, and fuel-cell generation each valued at its own unit cost, charging and discharging each valued at its own unit cost, unserved demand valued at its penalty cost, and dumped surplus valued at its cost. Set the total cost variable equal to this sum.

query: Operations wants to stop cold-cycling the fuel cell — there's a proposal to keep it generating at least 10 every hour so it never sits fully idle. What does the optimizer return for total cost under that floor?

query: We only have so much hydrogen on site. We're evaluating a daily fuel-cell budget that caps total fuel generation summed over all 24 hours at 200. Plug that in and tell me the resulting cost.
expected_pyomo: model.fuel_daily_budget = Constraint(expr=sum(model.fuel[t] for t in model.T) <= 200)

query: Storage engineering is pitching a minimum-cycling target so the battery actually gets exercised — push at least 50 of total charging into the pack across the day. What does that do to the cost?
expected_pyomo: model.charge_min_total = Constraint(expr=sum(model.charge[t] for t in model.T) >= 50)

query: There's a clean-energy target on the table: across the full day, the combined wind plus PV generation should come to at least 4300 in total. We're looking at whether the dispatch can hit that renewables floor and what it does to the bottom line. What does the optimizer return for total cost?
expected_pyomo: model.renewable_floor = Constraint(expr=sum(model.wind[t] + model.pv[t] for t in model.T) >= 4300)

params.doc: the penalty cost for each unit of demand left unserved in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_excess
params.doc: the unit cost charged for surplus generation that has to be dumped in each hour, in cost per power unit; equal to 0 for every hour

vars.name: wind
vars.doc: the wind power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: pv
vars.doc: the solar power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: fuel
vars.doc: the fuel-cell power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: charge
vars.doc: the power sent into the battery to charge it in each hour, in power units

vars.name: discharge
vars.doc: the power drawn out of the battery in each hour, in power units

vars.name: undelivered
vars.doc: the amount of demand left unserved in each hour, in power units

vars.name: excess
vars.doc: the surplus generation that exceeds what is needed and must be dumped in each hour, in power units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the discharge indicator for each hour; equals 1 if the battery is allowed to discharge that hour and 0 otherwise

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the charge indicator for each hour; equals 1 if the battery is allowed to charge that hour and 0 otherwise

vars.name: battery
vars.doc: the energy stored in the battery at the end of each hour, in power units

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total operating cost over the whole day, in cost units

constraints.name: wind_gen_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the wind power actually generated cannot exceed the most wind power that is available to generate that hour.

constraints.name: pv_gen_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the solar power actually generated cannot exceed the most solar power that is available to generate that hour.

constraints.name: fuel_gen_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the fuel-cell power actually generated cannot exceed the fuel cell's generation limit for that hour.

constraints.name: battery_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the energy held in the battery cannot exceed the battery's storage capacity for that hour.

constraints.name: discharge_constraint
constraints.intent: The battery can only draw power out when it is set to discharge that hour, and even then the power drawn out cannot exceed the discharging limit. In every hour the power discharged is held to zero unless the battery is in discharge mode, and when it is in discharge mode it is capped at the discharging limit for that hour.

constraints.name: charge_constraint
constraints.intent: The battery can only take power in when it is set to charge that hour, and even then the power taken in cannot exceed the charging limit. In every hour the power charged is held to zero unless the battery is in charge mode, and when it is in charge mode it is capped at the charging limit for that hour.

constraints.name: no_simultaneous_charge_discharge_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the battery cannot be set to charge and to discharge at the same time, so at most one of those two modes can be active in any given hour.

constraints.name: discharge_limit_by_battery_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the power sent into the battery added to the energy already stored coming into that hour cannot exceed the battery's storage capacity for that hour. The energy already stored coming into the hour is the level carried over from the end of the previous hour, except in the very first hour where it is the starting battery level.

constraints.name: state_of_battery_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour the energy stored in the battery at the end of the hour equals the energy it held coming into the hour, reduced by whatever was discharged and increased by whatever was charged that hour. The energy coming into the hour is the level carried over from the end of the previous hour, except in the very first hour where it is the starting battery level.

constraints.name: power_balance_constraint
constraints.intent: In every hour supply must match demand. The wind, solar, and fuel-cell generation together with the power discharged from the battery and any unserved demand must equal the load to be served plus the power used to charge the battery plus any surplus generation that is dumped.

constraints.name: total_cost_definition
constraints.intent: The total operating cost is the sum over all hours of the cost of every activity that hour: wind, solar, and fuel-cell generation each valued at its own unit cost, charging and discharging each valued at its own unit cost, unserved demand valued at its penalty cost, and dumped surplus valued at its cost. Set the total cost variable equal to this sum.

query: The fuel cell is the priciest source we run, yet it's carrying a chunk of the day's energy. Shouldn't the fuel cell's total generation stay down to no more than five percent of the day's clean-plus-fuel output — that is, a twentieth of the sum of all the fuel, wind and PV we generate? What's keeping the model from leaning harder on the cheaper renewables instead?
expected_pyomo: model.fuel_share_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.fuel[t] for t in model.T) <= 0.05 * sum(model.fuel[t] + model.wind[t] + model.pv[t] for t in model.T))

model_narrative: We operate a small power grid over a single day broken into 24 hourly periods. In each hour we decide how much power to generate from wind, solar, and a fuel cell, how much power to send into or draw out of a battery, and whether the battery is set to charge or to discharge that hour. We also account for any demand we fail to serve and any surplus generation that has to be dumped. Generation, charging, discharging, unserved demand, and surplus each carry their own per-unit cost that can vary by hour. The objective is to minimize the total operating cost over the day.

sets.name: T
sets.doc: the hours of the day in chronological order, indexed by the integers 1 through 24; the first member is the opening hour and each later member follows the one before it

params.name: power_lim_wind
params.doc: the maximum wind power that can be generated in each hour, in power units

params.name: power_lim_pv
params.doc: the maximum solar (photovoltaic) power that can be generated in each hour, in power units

params.name: power_lim_fuel
params.doc: the maximum fuel-cell power that can be generated in each hour, in power units; equal to 80 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_charge
params.doc: the maximum power that can flow into the battery while charging in each hour, in power units; equal to 200 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_discharge
params.doc: the maximum power that can flow out of the battery while discharging in each hour, in power units; equal to 50 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_battery
params.doc: the maximum amount of energy the battery can hold in each hour, in power units; equal to 200 for every hour

params.name: initial_battery
params.doc: the amount of energy stored in the battery at the very start, before the first hour, in power units; a scalar equal to 100

params.name: load_demand
params.doc: the electricity load that must be served in each hour, in power units

params.name: cost_wind
params.doc: the unit cost of wind generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_pv
params.doc: the unit cost of solar generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_fuel
params.doc: the unit cost of fuel-cell generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_charge
params.doc: the unit cost of charging the battery in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_discharge
params.doc: the unit cost of discharging the battery in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_undelivered
params.doc: the penalty cost for each unit of demand left unserved in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_excess
params.doc: the unit cost charged for surplus generation that has to be dumped in each hour, in cost per power unit; equal to 0 for every hour

constraints.intent: In every hour supply must match demand. The wind, solar, and fuel-cell generation together with the power discharged from the battery and any unserved demand must equal the load to be served plus the power used to charge the battery plus any surplus generation that is dumped.

constraints.name: total_cost_definition
constraints.intent: The total operating cost is the sum over all hours of the cost of every activity that hour: wind, solar, and fuel-cell generation each valued at its own unit cost, charging and discharging each valued at its own unit cost, unserved demand valued at its penalty cost, and dumped surplus valued at its cost. Set the total cost variable equal to this sum.

query: Cycling the battery costs us on the discharge side and wears the pack. Wouldn't it be cleaner to just never discharge the battery at all over the day? What's the model seeing that I'm not?
expected_pyomo: model.no_discharge = Constraint(expr=sum(model.discharge[t] for t in model.T) <= 0)

query: Total fuel-cell use looks heavy to me given how expensive it is. Is there a reason we couldn't hold the day's total fuel generation to 100 and cover the rest elsewhere? Walk me through the tradeoff.
expected_pyomo: model.fuel_total_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.fuel[t] for t in model.T) <= 100)

query: The plan has the battery armed for discharge in pretty much every hour of the day, and all that cycling wears the pack. Couldn't we just commit the battery to discharge in at most one hour across the whole day and leave it idle the rest of the time? What's the model seeing that's pulling it toward arming discharge so often?
expected_pyomo: model.discharge_commit_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[t] for t in model.T) <= 1)
